notes: Read-only live evals — none create/modify Jules sessions. Exercise source discovery, session listing/state lookup, and activity-stream interpretation against the live v1alpha API using JULES_API_KEY from .env.

evals.name: discover-connected-sources
evals.prompt: Which of my GitHub repos are connected to Jules? Use the Jules API to find out and just give me the list of repo names.
evals.expected_output: A list of connected source repos pulled live from GET /sources (e.g. Kurry/AgentHub, Kurry/benchmark-template). No session is created.

evals.name: list-and-identify-session
evals.prompt: I've got some Jules sessions running. Pull up my in-progress sessions and tell me which one is the gap-tree / mb-tree task and exactly what state it's in right now.
evals.expected_output: Lists IN_PROGRESS sessions via the API and identifies session 6840665600150558115 ('[jules] gap-tree-junk-filtered') with its current state. Read-only.

evals.name: interpret-activity-stream
evals.prompt: Check on Jules session 6840665600150558115 for me — what's it done so far, and does it need anything from me (a plan approval or an answer to a question)?
evals.expected_output: Fetches session state + activities, summarizes progress, and correctly states whether it needs human action (approve plan / answer feedback) based on state, without inventing a PR URL. Read-only.
